footer.tagline: Helping non-technical users choose the right cloud provider with AI-powered guidance and plain-English explanations.
footer.copyright: © {{year}} CloudAdvisor. Pricing estimates are approximate. Always verify with official provider documentation.

typing: Answer 6 questions. Get an AI-powered cloud recommendation.
AWS, GCP, Azure — find out which one is right for you.
Stop guessing. Start building on the right cloud.
From free tier to enterprise scale, we score it all.
The fastest way to pick your cloud provider.
home.feature1: 6-step guided wizard — done in under 2 minutes
home.step01Desc: Tell us what you're building, your budget, and what matters most to you.
home.step02Desc: Our scoring engine rates all 8 providers — AWS, GCP, Azure, DigitalOcean, Vercel, Netlify, Render, and Cloudflare — across 5 weighted dimensions.
home.step03Desc: An AI assistant explains the recommendation in plain English and answers your follow-up questions.
home.browseSubtitle: Explore full profiles, pros/cons, and feature checklists for every platform we evaluate.
home.resumeHasResults: You have a saved recommendation — want to view it?
home.resumeInProgress: You have a wizard session in progress — continue from step {{step}}?

shortcuts.title: What are you building?
shortcuts.subtitle: Pick a starting point and we'll pre-fill the wizard for you.
shortcuts.hostWebsiteDesc: Static site, landing page, or portfolio
shortcuts.trainMLDesc: AI, machine learning, or data science
shortcuts.mobileBackendDesc: API and data layer for iOS/Android
shortcuts.somethingElseDesc: Not sure yet? We'll help you figure it out

wizard.resetMessage: All your answers will be cleared.

step1.title: What are you building?
step1.subtitle: Pick the option that best describes your project.
step1.mobileDesc: API and data for iOS / Android
step1.otherDesc: Not sure yet — we'll help figure it out

step2.title: Who are you?
step2.subtitle: This helps us calibrate ease-of-use and support recommendations.
step2.studentDesc: Taking a course or building a portfolio project

step3.title: What's your monthly budget?
step3.subtitle: This is an estimate — you can always scale up later.
step3.freeDesc: I need to get started at $0

step4.title: What matters most?
step4.subtitle: Pick up to 2 priorities for your project.
step4.costDesc: Keep the bill as small as possible
step4.easeDesc: Simple UI, great docs, gentle learning curve
step4.scalabilityDesc: Ready to handle sudden traffic spikes
step4.supportDesc: Real humans available when things break

step5.title: Where are your users?
step5.subtitle: Geographic coverage affects both performance and compliance.
step5.singleDesc: My users are all in the same country
step5.multipleDesc: Users across a few different regions
step5.globalDesc: Users everywhere — latency matters a lot
step5.specificDesc: Data must stay in EU, US, or Asia

step6.title: Any specific services needed?
step6.subtitle: Select all that apply — this sharpens the recommendation.

results.title: Your cloud recommendation
results.subtitle: Scored across 5 dimensions — ranked best to worst for your project.
results.noResultsMessage: Complete the 6-step wizard to get your personalized cloud provider recommendation.
results.aiExplanationFallback: Add an OPENAI_API_KEY to the server .env to enable personalized explanations.

compare.title: Compare providers
compare.subtitleFromResults: Pre-populated with your top 3 results. All 8 providers available — toggle to swap any out.
compare.subtitleDefault: Select 2–3 providers from all 8 to compare side-by-side.
compare.selectAtLeast2: Select at least 2 providers above to see the comparison.

pricing.title: Pricing Estimator
pricing.subtitle: Adjust the sliders to estimate your monthly cloud bill across all 8 providers.
pricing.adjustSliders: Adjust the sliders to see estimated costs.
pricing.fetchError: Failed to calculate estimates. Is the server running?
pricing.computeNote: ⚡ For Vercel, Netlify, and Cloudflare, hours are converted to estimated function invocations (1 hr ≈ 5,000 requests — realistic average for typical web apps).
pricing.computeHint0: No compute — good for static sites or serverless-only.
pricing.computeHintLow: {{n}} hrs = {{pct}}% of one server running 24/7 for a month.
pricing.computeHintHigh: {{n}} hrs = {{x}}× the cost of one always-on server.
pricing.storageHintXS: {{n}} GB — suitable for small apps, logs, or a few media files.
pricing.storageHintMd: {{n}} GB — mid-range; covers most production apps.
pricing.storageHintLg: {{n}} GB — large dataset or media-heavy workload.
pricing.bandwidthHint0: No outbound transfer — good for internal or low-traffic apps.
pricing.bandwidthHintXS: {{n}} GB — typical for a low-traffic website or API.
pricing.bandwidthHintMd: {{n}} GB — moderate traffic; egress costs vary widely by provider.
pricing.bandwidthHintLg: {{n}} GB — high traffic; Cloudflare R2 has zero egress fees here.
pricing.databasesHint0: No managed databases — using a self-managed DB or none.
pricing.databasesHint1: 1 managed database — standard for most web apps.

glossary.title: Cloud Glossary
glossary.subtitle: {{count}} plain-English definitions — from complete beginner to advanced cloud concepts.
glossary.noMatch: No terms match "{{query}}". Try a different search.

notFound.heading: Page not found
notFound.message: This page doesn't exist. It may have been moved or the link might be wrong.

onboarding.title: New here? Find your cloud in 2 min
onboarding.message: Answer 6 quick questions — our scoring engine ranks all 8 providers for your exact use case.

error.title: Something went wrong
error.message: An unexpected error occurred. This is usually temporary — try refreshing the page or returning to the home page.

chat.title: CloudAdvisor AI
chat.starterMessage: Hi! Ask me anything about cloud providers, pricing, architecture, or which platform fits your project.
chat.apiKeyMissingMessage: Add OPENAI_API_KEY to server/.env to enable AI chat.
chat.questionGetStarted: How do I get started with {{name}}?
chat.questionHiddenCosts: What are the hidden costs I should know about?
chat.questionBeginner: Is {{name}} right for a complete beginner?
chat.questionDifference: What's the difference between AWS, GCP, and Azure?
chat.questionStartup: Which cloud provider is best for a small startup?
chat.questionServerless: How do I choose between serverless and containers?
chat.questionWebApp: What cloud services do I need to host a web app?

scoreBreakdown.useCaseWhy: How well this provider's strengths align with your chosen use case (website, web app, ML, etc.). Worth up to 30 points.
scoreBreakdown.budgetWhy: How well the provider's pricing model and tiers match your declared monthly budget. Worth up to 25 points.
scoreBreakdown.easeOfUseWhy: How approachable the platform is for your experience level — from individual developer to enterprise team. Worth up to 20 points.
scoreBreakdown.geographyWhy: How many regions this provider has and how well their infrastructure matches the geographic market you selected. Worth up to 15 points.
scoreBreakdown.servicesWhy: How many of your required services (databases, CDN, ML APIs, auth, etc.) this provider natively offers. Worth up to 10 points.
scoreBreakdown.hint: Hover any dimension label to see how it was scored.

pricingBreakdown.freeTierNote: ✓ Current usage falls within the free tier.
pricingBreakdown.freeTier12Month: Free tier is valid for the first 12 months only — standard rates apply after.
pricingBreakdown.cloudflareBandwidth: Zero egress fees from R2 — no data transfer charges. A major cost advantage over AWS, GCP, and Azure.
pricingBreakdown.renderTier: Free tier web services spin down after 15 min of inactivity and cold-start on the next request.
pricingBreakdown.disclaimer: Estimates based on standard on-demand rates. Reserved instances, committed-use discounts, and volume pricing are not modelled. Always verify costs with official provider calculators.
